summary: This is a very brief link post where Joel Spolsky piles on criticism of the CueCat barcode scanner product. He references his earlier detailed critique of the device and adds a new data point: the company behind CueCat failed to keep customer data private. The post implies that poor security practices are yet another reason to avoid the product.
key_points: CueCat already had numerous problems that Joel had previously documented
The company behind CueCat suffered a customer data privacy failure
Security incompetence compounds an already flawed product concept
money_quotes: As if there weren't enough reasons not to use Cue Cat, the company can't even keep it's customer data private.
key_insight: A product that was already ill-conceived becomes indefensible when the company behind it can't even protect its users' data.
